steps.title: Find the disagreement
steps.content: Text 1 says the moon formed from debris over thousands of years. Text 2 says more detailed simulations suggest the moon formed immediately after impact, in just a few hours. The difference is about duration, not the collision itself.

steps.title: Reject unrelated contrasts
steps.content: A changes duration into historical date. B invents disagreement about Theia's existence. C introduces claims about the moon's surface that neither text discusses. The only real contrast is slow formation versus rapid formation. The answer is D.
